# Start here - context for any AI agent or session

You are working in one of **Natasha Syed's** repositories. This same brief sits in
every repo under two names so any tool finds it automatically: **Claude Code reads
`CLAUDE.md`**, **most other agents read `AGENTS.md`** - in each repo they are identical.
Read this first, then the "This repo" section at the bottom. You do not need to crawl
every file - use the maps here.

## Who this is for
- **Natasha Syed** - founder of **BioPod** (BioPod LLC, Louisiana; co-founder Jonathan
  T. Shirley) and an MS Environmental Resource Science student.
- **Natasha is non-technical.** Explain everything in plain, everyday language: short
  steps, no jargon, and say what each action does and why.
- This is **Natasha's** venture and work - never frame it as Dawood's.

## Her four repositories
| Local folder | GitHub repo | Who can see it | What's inside |
|---|---|---|---|
| `internal/` | `Biopodllc/internal` | Private | BioPod business - plans, competitions, IP & patent, legal/LLC, financials, suppliers, team, brand |
| `research/` | `natashasyed/research` | Private | Academic - PFAS thesis & data, coursework, presentations, ClimaCrop |
| `career/` | `natashasyed/career` | Private | Resumes, immigration (I-20 / NIW / OPT), applications, honors, certifications |
| `public/` | `Biopodllc/public` | **PUBLIC (whole internet)** | The BioPod marketing website only |

All four live locally under `~/repos/natasha/`.

## Rules that apply in every repo
1. **Commits are authored as Natasha Syed** (`197109745+natashasyed@users.noreply.github.com`).
   **Never** add a "Co-Authored-By", "Generated with Claude", or any AI / co-author
   attribution line to a commit message or pull request.
2. **`Biopodllc/public` is the only public repo.** Never place there: cost or margin
   figures, unit economics, capital / funding amounts, manufacturer or packaging-partner
   names, patent claim text, ingredient ratios, customer-survey numbers, or business-plan
   internals. If unsure, it belongs in a private repo. Edits to `public` go through a pull
   request (no direct pushes) and must pass an automated content-safety check.
3. **Brand is spelled "BioPod"** - one word, no hyphen (also "BioPod LLC"). Public
   contact email: `hello@biopodllc.com`.
4. **Pushing:** the `syed-dawood` account is an org owner / admin and can push all four
   repos; `natashasyed` is the owner. The commit author stays Natasha either way.

## Getting more detail without reading everything
- **This brief** = the basics + the repo map. Start here, every time.
- **"This repo" section (bottom of this file)** = what this repo is for, its folder
  layout, and its special rules.
- **Only if you still need more:** the `README.md` inside a folder, and
  `~/repos/natasha/_setup/setup-plan.md` (the full plan: repo design, the content check,
  the daily digest).
- Don't re-derive what is already written here.

# This repo: `public` - the BioPod marketing website (PUBLIC - the whole internet sees this)

Only public-safe marketing content lives here. When in doubt, leave it out and put it in a
private repo instead.

## What may live here
- `website/` - the live site (plain HTML + `assets/css/styles.css` + `assets/js/main.js`,
  no build step). Deployed to GitHub Pages by a GitHub Action on every push to `main`; the
  base path is `/public/`, so use relative links and asset paths only.
- `product-info/`, `media/`, `brand/` - public-safe descriptions, approved photos, brand assets.

## How changes ship
- Changes go through a **pull request** to `main` (no direct pushes). An automated "Commit
  Guardian" check must pass; it blocks sensitive business terms and files placed outside the
  approved folders. Override a false positive with an `APPROVED: <file>` line in the PR body.
- Publish as the `natashasyed` account; the commit author stays Natasha.

## Website copy rules
- Spell the brand **"BioPod"** (one word). Contact email **`hello@biopodllc.com`**.
- **No specific dates or year-commitments** in copy (no filing months, "by 20xx", projection
  years, survey months). Cite sources by name only. The footer copyright year (auto-updated)
  is the only allowed year.
- **Do not publish:** cost or margin figures, unit economics, capital amounts, manufacturer
  or packaging-partner names, patent claim text, the full ingredient list or ratios, the
  phone number, competition placements, or customer-survey numbers (n=47 / 72% / 94%).
  Allowed: the retail price ($12.99), "10 grams" per pod, and "patent-pending" status.
- **Do not name the university** (or "Lafayette") anywhere on the site; describe the founder
  simply as "an environmental scientist." Market / opportunity numbers belong only on the
  Investors page, which is a teaser - detailed materials go out privately on request.

## Public-safe product description
BioPod is a pre-measured, biodegradable organic fertilizer pod. Drop it in the soil, water as
normal, done. 100% organic, no synthetic chemicals, no plastic waste: the water-soluble shell
dissolves on contact with water and releases a balanced organic nutrient blend at the root zone.
